item.title: No Strings Attached: An Improvised Puppet Show for Grownups
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

At Unexpected Productions’ Market Theater\, puppets let their hair down (or strings loose) in No Strings Attached—a hilarious\, adults improv puppet show. After a long day entertaining the kids\, these puppets are ready to play—singing bawdy songs\, cracking jokes\, and getting up to all kinds of outrageous fun. And the best part? You call the shots with your suggestions! \nDon’t miss this one-of-a-kind comedy show at Pike Place Market. It’s puppets\, it’s improv\, and it’s guaranteed to make you laugh. \nTHIS SHOW CONTAINS MATURE CONTENT\, LANGUAGE\, AND SITUATIONS

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: Taste of Improv - Improv Open Mic Georgetown Art Attack
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Seattle Improv School\, 5700 6th Ave S\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Whether you’re a student\, a pro\, or just checking out the Art Attack\, this is your chance to take the stage\, make bold choices\, and have a blast doing it.\n\nJoin Unexpected Productions Improv School as we join Georgetown’s ART ATTACK with a spontaneous showcase of improv talent. Bring your squad\, support each other\, and celebrate the joy of live\, unfiltered performance.\nHit the Georgetown Art Attack\, then head over to the Improv School.\nCome for the art. Stay for the improv. See you there!

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: Seattle Suites: An Improvised Neil Simon Comedy
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Improvised comedy in the style of Neil Simon\, where one hotel suite becomes a comedic pressure cooker for 3 hilarious improvised tales One hotel suite. Countless misunderstandings. Relentless laughs. Seattle Suite is a laugh-driven\, fully improvised play inspired by the style of Plaza Suite and California Suite\, capturing the signature humor of Neil Simon that is fast\, verbal\, and rooted in beautifully human flaws. Each night\, a single Seattle hotel room becomes the setting for interconnected scenes inspired by your suggestions\, friends overshare and spiral\, romantic gestures go wrong\, awkward silences stretch\, and small problems become big ones. The comedy comes from character and relationship\, building through sharp dialogue\, escalating tension\, and recognizable\, lived-in situations. Perfect for fans of smart comedy\, relationship humor\, and classic theatrical wit.

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: Seattle Theatresports 2026
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 9:30 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Seattle’s high-energy improv battle where your suggestions shape every scene. Fast\, funny\, unpredictable comedy Fri. & Sat. 9:30 Welcome to Seattle’s funnest improv comedy show\, a high-energy\, no-rules showdown where your ideas shape every scene. Seattle Theatresports at Unexpected Productions’ Market Theater in Pike Place Market is part comedy battle\, part game night\, and completely powered by the crowd. Boo the judges. Cheer the Gum Wall race and laugh til it hurts! Every show\, two teams of top Seattle improvisers go head to head\, performing games\, stories\, characters\, and scenes inspired entirely by your suggestions. Judges score the rounds\, decide the winners\, and shape the fun (as we boo them). No scripts. No safety nets. Just fast thinking\, bold choices\, and pure creative mayhem. This isn’t your parents’ improv show\, unless your parents were super cool.

From Unexpected Productions

item.title: Show Your Game: An Improvised Game Show
item.description:
Sunday, March 15, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Ever wonder what secret skills your neighbors are hiding? That quiet person next to you on the bus might be a brain surgeon\, a barbecue champion\, or the only parent in town who can get their toddler to nap on time. Show Your Game is here to celebrate those everyday experts and unexpected talents! \nEach night\, a hilarious mix of improvisers and brave audience members go head-to-head\, diving deep into the worlds of two surprising experts. They’ll tackle quirky questions and wild challenges\, each crafted by the experts themselves to test just how much our contestants really know. \nLove the clever chaos of QI\, the unpredictable twists of Game Changer\, or the absurd fun of Taskmaster? Then this is your kind of show. \nSo\, when it’s your turn to step into someone else’s world… are you ready to Show Your Game?

From Unexpected Productions

item.title: NOCHE DE IMPROV EN ESPAÑOL
item.description:
Monday, March 16, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions Improv School ISSAQUAH/EASTSIDE\, 1315 NW Mall Street\, Suite 4\, Issaquah\, WA\, 98108\, United States

Noche de Improvisación en español\, es una noche de jam para los que hablan FLUIDAMENTE español\, que ya sean estudiantes actuales de UP\, exalumnos de UP\, miembros del conjunto y exalumnos\, para jugar juntos y seguir desarrollando sus habilidades de improvisación en español. Noche de Improvisación en español \, está dirigida por Laila Valencia. Los juegos ayudan a los improvisadores a desarrollar habilidades\, desarrollar confianza y fomentar la creatividad. Pero lo mas importante es … ¡es en español y es DIVERTIDO! El espacio es limitado a 16 personas. Fecha y hora: El tercer lunes de cada mes\, de 7 a 9 p. m. Ubicación: 650 S Orcas Suite 101\, Seattle\, WA 98108 Precio: $10 (11.24 con cargos) El Requisito Previo: Unexpected Productions Improv 100 Laila Valencia Nació en Guadalajara Jalisco México finales de los años 70as\, donde vivió toda su infancia y adolescencia\, siempre tuvo interés por las artes escénicas y participó en varios proyectos de teatro y danza contemporanea en la UDG \, a finales de los 90tas\, Se va a vivir a Seattle WA donde conoce la técnica de comedia en improvisación \, de la cual se enamora se inscribe en clases y talleres especializados\,con maestros como Keith Johnstone creador de Theatresports\, Gorilla and Maestro improv shows\, Randy Dixon director artistico de Unexpected Produccions desde hace 20 anos\, talleres con Gary Austin\, Asiste a retiros de improvisacion en Nueva York con artistic new direcctins y desde entonces a participado en 6 festivales internacionales de improvisación\,A Impartido clases para toda las edades\, ahora es miembro de de la compañía más antigua de improvisacionnen Seattle Unexpected Productions\, en la cual continúa su preparación y actúa dos o mas veces por semana \, siempre está trabajando en proyectos diferentes\, forma larga y corta de improv\, contando historias que se inspiradan con las ideas del público.

From Unexpected Productions

item.title: PETS: Southwood Elementary School
item.description:
Tuesday, March 17, 2026 at 1:15 PM

Location: Taproot Theatre, 204 N 85th St, Seattle, WA 98103

Written By Melanie Godsey For grades K–5 | 1-hour presentation including performance and actor-led talkback It’s a tail-wagging time at Pet School, where Biscuit the pup and all their furry and feathered friends learn to sit, stay, and help a human in need. When things get ruff for Biscuit’s best human friend, Corey, it’s time to fetch help. A once-close friendship has gone to the dogs, and the bus stop becomes a bullying zone. Using every paws-itively clever trick from Pet School, Biscuit will stop at nothing to help Corey and teach the other kids that being an upstander is the best trick of all. Education Goals: Bullying prevention Turning bystanders into upstanders Overcoming failure Teamwork

From Taproot Theatre

Event details

item.title: 700 Level Student Showcase
item.description:
Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Witness the creation of believable characters\, inspiring scenes\, and some improv magic by 700 level class.\n\n\n\nJoin the advanced students in our 700 class and witness them blossom with new improv skills. Under the guidance of our expert improv teachers\, 700 students are learning new narrative formats and enhancing their skills by working in an ensemble. You get to see the fruits of their efforts by witnessing the creation of believable characters\, inspiring scenes\, and some improv magic.

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: Duo Comedy Showcase 2026
item.description:
Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Seattle’s one and only open mic dedicated to two-person improv teams! Every week at Unexpected Productions\, two-person teams take the stage to create hilarious\, fast-paced\, totally unscripted comedy right before your eyes. No scripts\, no safety nets—just pure improv magic. \nAt Duos\, everyone shines. Newer performers sharpen their game. Seasoned improvisers experiment\, take risks\, and get wonderfully weird. The results? Quick-witted scenes\, surprising twists\, and bold choices that feel impossible until you watch them happen live. \nWhether you dream of being the next Key & Peele\, Tina Fey & Amy Poehler\, or a chaotic tag-team like Deadpool & Wolverine\, this is your chance to take the mic and run with it. All experience levels are welcome. Bring a friend\, form a team\, and give Seattle a show they won’t forget. To sign up your duo\, email duoshowcase@gmail.com. \nLooking for a midweek serotonin boost? Watching the show is just as fun as performing in it. Grab a seat\, grab a friend\, and discover why Unexpected Productions is the home of Seattle improv.

From Unexpected Productions

item.title: Ain't Misbehavin'
item.description:
Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Taproot Theatre, 204 N 85th St, Seattle, WA 98103

The Fats Waller Music Show by Richard Maltby Jr. The Fats Waller Musical Show The club is alive, the band is swingin’, and the music of a jazz legend brings the house down. This immersive musical revue celebrates the life of Fats Waller on stage with joy, rhythm, and soul. Remembering his generation-defining songs and lasting impact on today’s music, this exuberant Tony Award-winning tribute to Black artistry sings, laughs, hopes, and lives out loud.

From Taproot Theatre

Event details

item.title: Loose Cannons 2026
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Loose Cannons is Seattle improv at its most unpredictable. Every performance at Unexpected Productions in Pike Place Market is a one-of-a-kind experience: exciting\, unexpected\, a little dangerous\, and always ridiculously fun. This is the show where anything can happen and usually does. \nPart One: Part One: The night kicks off with a unique performance\, setting the performers in a new format with special guest improvisers showcasing their signature style or a never-before-seen format. Your suggestions fuel the fire\, and the improvisers will ignite the stage\, bringing your ideas to life. \nPart Two: The second half launches from a single audience suggestion\, setting the performers in motion. It might start with a song\, a monologue\, or a rapid-fire group connection game. Whatever emerges will inspire spontaneous stories\, surprising twists\, and big comedic swings. \nLoose Cannons is fueled by audience ideas\, fearless performers\, and the thrill of not knowing what happens next. You light the fuse. They run with it. What they create is entirely up to you.

From Unexpected Productions

item.title: IMPROV 100 SATURDAYS- Intro to Improv: Say Yes!\, SPRING'26
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions Improv School ISSAQUAH/EASTSIDE\, 1315 NW Mall Street\, Suite 4\, Issaquah\, WA\, 98108\, United States

Discover Unexpected Productions’ philosophy of improvisation\, developed over our 43 years of improvising in Seattle! You’ll learn how to actively listen\, to use your own life to create collaborative stories\, and you’ll work on the fundamental principles of improvisation: what it means to say\, “Yes\, And”\, how to tell stories that include everyone in the room\, how to stop worrying about failing\, and the games and concepts behind what we do on stages around the world! Under the guidance of one of our best Seattle improv teachers\, you will learn to:· Be a Better Listener Conquer Fears Spark Creativity Meet New People & Make New Friends Become a Better Problem Solver Gain Confidence· Fail With Grace (and Maybe Even Enjoy It) Say Yes More in Your Life Get Out of Your Comfort Zone Become a Better Public Speaker Discover More About Yourself …and have a blast doing it! Time and Dates: Saturdays for eight (8) weeks\, from March 21 to May 9\, 12pm to 2:30pm. NOTE: Attendance at the first session of your course is mandatory as it sets the agenda for the entire quarter. If you think you will have to miss the first class\, please consider registering for the next quarter. No Prerequisites. All students begin at this level\, even those with existing improv experience. It’s a prerequisite to the other levels. Location: 650 S Orcas St\, Room 217\, Seattle\, WA\, 98108 (In Georgetown\, south of downtown\, with plenty of parking.) Maximum Students: 16 Cost: Ticket price $295.00 Age: 18 and above **Cancellation policy: If you must cancel before the first class\, we can only offer credit towards the same level class next quarter. We are unable to offer refunds. If you cancel after the first class we cannot offer credit towards a future class. Again\, we are unable to offer refunds.

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

From Unexpected Productions

item.title: Seattle Theatresports 2026
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 9:30 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Seattle’s high-energy improv battle where your suggestions shape every scene. Fast\, funny\, unpredictable comedy Fri. & Sat. 9:30 Welcome to Seattle’s funnest improv comedy show\, a high-energy\, no-rules showdown where your ideas shape every scene. Seattle Theatresports at Unexpected Productions’ Market Theater in Pike Place Market is part comedy battle\, part game night\, and completely powered by the crowd. Boo the judges. Cheer the Gum Wall race and laugh til it hurts! Every show\, two teams of top Seattle improvisers go head to head\, performing games\, stories\, characters\, and scenes inspired entirely by your suggestions. Judges score the rounds\, decide the winners\, and shape the fun (as we boo them). No scripts. No safety nets. Just fast thinking\, bold choices\, and pure creative mayhem. This isn’t your parents’ improv show\, unless your parents were super cool.

From Unexpected Productions

item.title: IMPROV 100 SUNDAYS 3:30 pm- Intro to Improv: Say Yes!\, SPRING'26
item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 3:30 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions Improv School ISSAQUAH/EASTSIDE\, 1315 NW Mall Street\, Suite 4\, Issaquah\, WA\, 98108\, United States

Discover Unexpected Productions’ philosophy of improvisation\, developed over our 43 years of improvising in Seattle! You’ll learn how to actively listen\, to use your own life to create collaborative stories\, and you’ll work on the fundamental principles of improvisation: what it means to say\, “Yes\, And”\, how to tell stories that include everyone in the room\, how to stop worrying about failing\, and the games and concepts behind what we do on stages around the world! Under the guidance of one of our best Seattle improv teachers\, you will learn to:· Be a Better Listener Conquer Fears Spark Creativity Meet New People & Make New Friends Become a Better Problem Solver Gain Confidence· Fail With Grace (and Maybe Even Enjoy It) Say Yes More in Your Life Get Out of Your Comfort Zone Become a Better Public Speaker Discover More About Yourself …and have a blast doing it! Time and Dates: Sundays for eight (8) weeks\, from March 22 to May 27\, 3:30pm to 6:00pm (No class on April 5th) NOTE: Attendance at the first session of your course is mandatory as it sets the agenda for the entire quarter. If you think you will have to miss the first class\, please consider registering for the next quarter. No Prerequisites. All students begin at this level\, even those with existing improv experience. It’s a prerequisite to the other levels. Location: 5700 6th Ave St\, Suite 109\, Umbrella Room\, Seattle\, WA 98108 (In Georgetown\, south of downtown\, with plenty of parking.) Maximum Students: 16 Cost: Ticket price $295.00 Age: 18 and above **Cancellation policy: If you must cancel before the first class\, we can only offer credit towards the same level class next quarter. We are unable to offer refunds. If you cancel after the first class we cannot offer credit towards a future class. Again\, we are unable to offer refunds.

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: Show Your Game: An Improvised Game Show
item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Ever wonder what secret skills your neighbors are hiding? That quiet person next to you on the bus might be a brain surgeon\, a barbecue champion\, or the only parent in town who can get their toddler to nap on time. Show Your Game is here to celebrate those everyday experts and unexpected talents! \nEach night\, a hilarious mix of improvisers and brave audience members go head-to-head\, diving deep into the worlds of two surprising experts. They’ll tackle quirky questions and wild challenges\, each crafted by the experts themselves to test just how much our contestants really know. \nLove the clever chaos of QI\, the unpredictable twists of Game Changer\, or the absurd fun of Taskmaster? Then this is your kind of show. \nSo\, when it’s your turn to step into someone else’s world… are you ready to Show Your Game?

From Unexpected Productions

item.title: IMPROV 100 WEDNESDAYS - Intro to Improv: Say Yes!\, SPRING'26
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions Improv School ISSAQUAH/EASTSIDE\, 1315 NW Mall Street\, Suite 4\, Issaquah\, WA\, 98108\, United States

You are registered. Welcome! Thank you for registering for this eight-week course at Unexpected Productions Improv. We’re excited to have you join us. Please read the details below carefully. This information is important. Time and Dates: Wednesdays for eight (8) weeks\, from March 25 to May 23\, 7pm to 9:30pm Important Attendance Note Attendance at the first session is mandatory\, as it sets the agenda for the entire quarter. If you think you may miss the first class\, we strongly recommend registering for the next quarter instead. Location: 5700 6th Ave St\, Suite 109\, Umbrella Room\, Seattle\, WA 98108 Class Details • Maximum Students: 16 • Cost: $295 • Age Requirement: 18+ Cancellation Policy If you must cancel before the first class\, we can offer credit toward the same level class next quarter. We are unable to offer refunds. If you cancel after the first class\, we cannot issue credit for a future class and cannot offer refunds. If you have questions before the first class\, please contact us at school@unexpectedproductions.org We look forward to working with you and can’t wait to get started. Improv classes are physically active and often involve movement\, standing\, and high-energy exercises\, so staying hydrated is important. We recommend the following: • Comfortable clothing that allows for easy movement • Comfortable shoes\, as you will likely be standing and moving for extended periods • A notebook and pen\, if you would like to take notes on techniques or exercises • A water bottle (some locations do not have water fountains) • A “Yes\, And” attitude. A willingness to participate\, take risks\, and be brave is the most important thing to bring

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: Duo Comedy Showcase 2026
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Seattle’s one and only open mic dedicated to two-person improv teams! Every week at Unexpected Productions\, two-person teams take the stage to create hilarious\, fast-paced\, totally unscripted comedy right before your eyes. No scripts\, no safety nets—just pure improv magic. \nAt Duos\, everyone shines. Newer performers sharpen their game. Seasoned improvisers experiment\, take risks\, and get wonderfully weird. The results? Quick-witted scenes\, surprising twists\, and bold choices that feel impossible until you watch them happen live. \nWhether you dream of being the next Key & Peele\, Tina Fey & Amy Poehler\, or a chaotic tag-team like Deadpool & Wolverine\, this is your chance to take the mic and run with it. All experience levels are welcome. Bring a friend\, form a team\, and give Seattle a show they won’t forget. To sign up your duo\, email duoshowcase@gmail.com. \nLooking for a midweek serotonin boost? Watching the show is just as fun as performing in it. Grab a seat\, grab a friend\, and discover why Unexpected Productions is the home of Seattle improv.

From Unexpected Productions

If you think you will have to miss the first class\, please consider registering for the next quarter. No Prerequisites. All students begin at this level\, even those with existing improv experience. It’s a prerequisite to the other levels. Location: 5700 6th Ave St\, Suite 109\, Umbrella Room\, Seattle\, WA 98108 (In Georgetown\, south of downtown\, with plenty of parking.) Maximum Students: 16 Cost: Ticket price $295.00 Age: 18 and above **Cancellation policy: If you must cancel before the first class\, we can only offer credit towards the same level class next quarter. We are unable to offer refunds. If you cancel after the first class we cannot offer credit towards a future class. Again\, we are unable to offer refunds.

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: Loose Cannons 2026
item.description:
Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Loose Cannons is Seattle improv at its most unpredictable. Every performance at Unexpected Productions in Pike Place Market is a one-of-a-kind experience: exciting\, unexpected\, a little dangerous\, and always ridiculously fun. This is the show where anything can happen and usually does. \nPart One: Part One: The night kicks off with a unique performance\, setting the performers in a new format with special guest improvisers showcasing their signature style or a never-before-seen format. Your suggestions fuel the fire\, and the improvisers will ignite the stage\, bringing your ideas to life. \nPart Two: The second half launches from a single audience suggestion\, setting the performers in motion. It might start with a song\, a monologue\, or a rapid-fire group connection game. Whatever emerges will inspire spontaneous stories\, surprising twists\, and big comedic swings. \nLoose Cannons is fueled by audience ideas\, fearless performers\, and the thrill of not knowing what happens next. You light the fuse. They run with it. What they create is entirely up to you.

From Unexpected Productions

item.title: Seattle Suites: An Improvised Neil Simon Comedy
item.description:
Friday, March 27, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Improvised comedy in the style of Neil Simon\, where one hotel suite becomes a comedic pressure cooker for 3 hilarious improvised tales One hotel suite. Countless misunderstandings. Relentless laughs. Seattle Suite is a laugh-driven\, fully improvised play inspired by the style of Plaza Suite and California Suite\, capturing the signature humor of Neil Simon that is fast\, verbal\, and rooted in beautifully human flaws. Each night\, a single Seattle hotel room becomes the setting for interconnected scenes inspired by your suggestions\, friends overshare and spiral\, romantic gestures go wrong\, awkward silences stretch\, and small problems become big ones. The comedy comes from character and relationship\, building through sharp dialogue\, escalating tension\, and recognizable\, lived-in situations. Perfect for fans of smart comedy\, relationship humor\, and classic theatrical wit.

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: Seattle Theatresports 2026
item.description:
Friday, March 27, 2026 at 9:30 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Seattle’s high-energy improv battle where your suggestions shape every scene. Fast\, funny\, unpredictable comedy Fri. & Sat. 9:30 Welcome to Seattle’s funnest improv comedy show\, a high-energy\, no-rules showdown where your ideas shape every scene. Seattle Theatresports at Unexpected Productions’ Market Theater in Pike Place Market is part comedy battle\, part game night\, and completely powered by the crowd. Boo the judges. Cheer the Gum Wall race and laugh til it hurts! Every show\, two teams of top Seattle improvisers go head to head\, performing games\, stories\, characters\, and scenes inspired entirely by your suggestions. Judges score the rounds\, decide the winners\, and shape the fun (as we boo them). No scripts. No safety nets. Just fast thinking\, bold choices\, and pure creative mayhem. This isn’t your parents’ improv show\, unless your parents were super cool.

From Unexpected Productions

item.title: Show Your Game: An Improvised Game Show
item.description:
Sunday, March 29, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Ever wonder what secret skills your neighbors are hiding? That quiet person next to you on the bus might be a brain surgeon\, a barbecue champion\, or the only parent in town who can get their toddler to nap on time. Show Your Game is here to celebrate those everyday experts and unexpected talents! \nEach night\, a hilarious mix of improvisers and brave audience members go head-to-head\, diving deep into the worlds of two surprising experts. They’ll tackle quirky questions and wild challenges\, each crafted by the experts themselves to test just how much our contestants really know. \nLove the clever chaos of QI\, the unpredictable twists of Game Changer\, or the absurd fun of Taskmaster? Then this is your kind of show. \nSo\, when it’s your turn to step into someone else’s world… are you ready to Show Your Game?

From Unexpected Productions

item.title: Ain't Misbehavin'
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Taproot Theatre, 204 N 85th St, Seattle, WA 98103

The Fats Waller Music Show by Richard Maltby Jr. The Fats Waller Musical Show The club is alive, the band is swingin’, and the music of a jazz legend brings the house down. This immersive musical revue celebrates the life of Fats Waller on stage with joy, rhythm, and soul. Remembering his generation-defining songs and lasting impact on today’s music, this exuberant Tony Award-winning tribute to Black artistry sings, laughs, hopes, and lives out loud.

From Taproot Theatre

item.title: Ain't Misbehavin'
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Taproot Theatre, 204 N 85th St, Seattle, WA 98103

The Fats Waller Music Show by Richard Maltby Jr. The Fats Waller Musical Show The club is alive, the band is swingin’, and the music of a jazz legend brings the house down. This immersive musical revue celebrates the life of Fats Waller on stage with joy, rhythm, and soul. Remembering his generation-defining songs and lasting impact on today’s music, this exuberant Tony Award-winning tribute to Black artistry sings, laughs, hopes, and lives out loud.

item.title: PETS: Park Orchard Elementary
item.description:
Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at 2:15 PM

Location: Taproot Theatre, 204 N 85th St, Seattle, WA 98103

Written By Melanie Godsey For grades K–5 | 1-hour presentation including performance and actor-led talkback It’s a tail-wagging time at Pet School, where Biscuit the pup and all their furry and feathered friends learn to sit, stay, and help a human in need. When things get ruff for Biscuit’s best human friend, Corey, it’s time to fetch help. A once-close friendship has gone to the dogs, and the bus stop becomes a bullying zone. Using every paws-itively clever trick from Pet School, Biscuit will stop at nothing to help Corey and teach the other kids that being an upstander is the best trick of all. Education Goals: Bullying prevention Turning bystanders into upstanders Overcoming failure Teamwork

From Taproot Theatre

The Fats Waller Music Show by Richard Maltby Jr. The Fats Waller Musical Show The club is alive, the band is swingin’, and the music of a jazz legend brings the house down. This immersive musical revue celebrates the life of Fats Waller on stage with joy, rhythm, and soul. Remembering his generation-defining songs and lasting impact on today’s music, this exuberant Tony Award-winning tribute to Black artistry sings, laughs, hopes, and lives out loud.

item.title: PETS
item.description:
Friday, April 24, 2026 at 2:30 PM

Location: Taproot Theatre, 204 N 85th St, Seattle, WA 98103

Chinook Elementary School Written By Melanie Godsey For grades K–5 | 1-hour presentation including performance and actor-led talkback It’s a tail-wagging time at Pet School, where Biscuit the pup and all their furry and feathered friends learn to sit, stay, and help a human in need. When things get ruff for Biscuit’s best human friend, Corey, it’s time to fetch help. A once-close friendship has gone to the dogs, and the bus stop becomes a bullying zone. Using every paws-itively clever trick from Pet School, Biscuit will stop at nothing to help Corey and teach the other kids that being an upstander is the best trick of all. Education Goals: Bullying prevention Turning bystanders into upstanders Overcoming failure Teamwork

From Taproot Theatre

item.title: Barefoot in the Park
item.description:
Wednesday, May 13, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Taproot Theatre, 204 N 85th St, Seattle, WA 98103

By Neil Simon They’ve just said, “I do,” but can their love handle five flights of stairs and a tiny New York apartment? A buttoned-up young lawyer and his free-spirited bride stumble into married life, eccentric neighbors, leaky ceilings, and romantic chaos. Full of wit, chemistry, and charm, this classic rom-com bubbles over with laughter and warmth. Love is messy—and that's what makes it fun.

item.title: Barefoot in the Park
item.description:
Saturday, May 23, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: Taproot Theatre, 204 N 85th St, Seattle, WA 98103

By Neil Simon They’ve just said, “I do,” but can their love handle five flights of stairs and a tiny New York apartment? A buttoned-up young lawyer and his free-spirited bride stumble into married life, eccentric neighbors, leaky ceilings, and romantic chaos. Full of wit, chemistry, and charm, this classic rom-com bubbles over with laughter and warmth. Love is messy—and that's what makes it fun.

item.title: Barefoot in the Park
item.description:
Wednesday, June 3, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: Taproot Theatre, 204 N 85th St, Seattle, WA 98103

By Neil Simon They’ve just said, “I do,” but can their love handle five flights of stairs and a tiny New York apartment? A buttoned-up young lawyer and his free-spirited bride stumble into married life, eccentric neighbors, leaky ceilings, and romantic chaos. Full of wit, chemistry, and charm, this classic rom-com bubbles over with laughter and warmth. Love is messy—and that's what makes it fun.

Location: Taproot Theatre, 204 N 85th St, Seattle, WA 98103

By Neil Simon They’ve just said, “I do,” but can their love handle five flights of stairs and a tiny New York apartment? A buttoned-up young lawyer and his free-spirited bride stumble into married life, eccentric neighbors, leaky ceilings, and romantic chaos. Full of wit, chemistry, and charm, this classic rom-com bubbles over with laughter and warmth. Love is messy—and that's what makes it fun.

From Taproot Theatre
